title: Llama7B physical HBM compute sensitivity
hypothesis: With merged all-measured local L1 costs and the physical high-SRAM HBM/NoC region, Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8 decode attention becomes memory/interconnect-bound only above a finite compute-throughput boundary; identifying that boundary prevents over-crediting small local compute arrays and guides the next memory/logic balance.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What is the smallest compute throughput that preserves the practical Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8 attention frontier under the high-SRAM physical HBM/NoC region?
include: Llama7B proxy decode attention with sequence length 131072, hidden size 4096, 32 layers, native GQA8 KV8
HBM configuration: 8 stacks, 16 pseudo-channels per stack, 64-bit pseudo-channel width, 9000 MT/s, 0.75 effective bandwidth efficiency
SRAM area fraction 0.75, usable SRAM fractions 0.70 and 0.85, local SRAM fractions 0.10, 0.25, and 0.50
Bank counts 16 and 64 with 32768 B/cycle NoC bandwidth and one modeled NoC hop
Compute sweep from 32768 to 524288 MAC/cycle and 8192 to 65536 vector ops/cycle
exclude: New RTL or PnR for larger global compute arrays
Approximate KV formats such as KV4 or MQA quality-changing variants
Full routed global NoC RTL and HBM controller timing
Training or QAT recovery for the Llama7B model itself
follow_on_broad_sweep: Add physically sized compute-array candidates near the boundary
Sweep global NoC hop count and bandwidth once the compute floor is known
Revisit SRAM/HBM tradeoffs for larger die sizes using the boundary compute points

expected_benefit: Converts the broad more-compute assumption into a measured boundary for the Llama7B 131k frontier model
Separates compute-bound small-array points from memory/interconnect-bound high-SRAM points
Guides whether the next expensive PPA runs should target compute scaling, NoC structure, or SRAM/HBM balance
risks: HBM and NoC behavior are analytic planning models, not full controller or routed NoC implementations
MAC/cycle and vector-op/cycle are throughput proxies until mapped to concrete clustered RTL
The quality assumption remains native GQA8 KV8; approximation recovery is not measured for full Llama7B training
The local L1 measured baseline is reused, so global command distribution and large-array timing are still estimated

required_evaluations.objective: Find the smallest compute throughput that remains memory/interconnect-bound for Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8 under the practical high-SRAM HBM/NoC region, using the merged all-measured local L1 baseline and physical HBM memory/NoC frontier as inputs.

expected_result.reason: The result should show where compute throughput stops dominating and HBM/SRAM/NoC service becomes the limiting resource for the 131k Llama7B attention schedule.

knowledge_refs: discussion:2026-06-03 compute throughput must be interpreted under practical physical constraints
discussion:2026-06-03 memory/logic balance for Llama7B 131k should be evaluated before committing to larger RTL arrays
